item.name: Dr. Martens Postal Supreme 4-Eye Shoe
item.materialsAndDetails: Smooth leather upper with debossed printed snakeskin pattern on the Snakeskin colorway, air-cushioned sole, padded collar, printed logos on insole, and side tab. Made exclusively for Supreme.

observations.details: Smooth leather upper with debossed printed snakeskin pattern, air-cushioned sole, padded collar, printed logos on insole and side tab, made exclusively for Supreme.

observations.interpretation: Black is the safe wearable pair; Snakeskin is the bolder texture-driven pair.

observations.context: Week 10 demand is split across Dr. Martens and the Ghostface capsule, with Sole Retriever calling the Dr. Martens collab a key item of the week.

observations.warning: Product-specific public pages had no visible completed-sale history at capture; API values are live ask/bid market formation, not completed sales.

dataQualityWarnings: StockX Postal product pages showed no visible completed-sale history at capture, so live earnMore values may reflect sellers anchoring high rather than cleared transactions.
Bid/ask spreads above $150 appear on most variants; high expected sale values should be treated as high-upside but thin-liquidity estimates.
No eBay completed-sale scrape was used for this first Doc Martens pass.
Supreme official page captured Snakeskin copy directly; Black colorway confirmation comes from Sneaker Bar Detroit and StockX.

model.name: transparent comparable-weighted baseline
model.target: expected resale sale price before seller fees by horizon

Snakeskin.liveOverrideNote: Snakeskin and Black are nearly even through sizes 6-12 in live earnMore, while Snakeskin 13 is a high outlier.

colors.title: Dr. Martens Postal Supreme 4-Eye Shoe Supreme Black
Dr. Martens Postal Supreme 4-Eye Shoe Supreme Snakeskin

method.detailedReasoning: The Doc Martens Postal forecast follows the Ghostface workflow: use live StockX earnMore as the day-1 expected sale anchor, sellFaster as the floor, and 12% marketplace fee plus $15 handling for net profit. The analyst pass keeps the strong day-1 upside but discounts confidence because the target product pages had no visible completed sales and most variants show very wide bid/ask spreads. Black is treated as the safest long-term color; Snakeskin earns a novelty premium only where live demand supports it, especially size 13, but high spread risk is called out explicitly.
reviewQuestionsForCritic: Are the StockX earnMore values too ask-stack-driven because the Postal product pages have no completed-sale history yet?
Should Snakeskin size 13 be capped lower until a tighter bid forms?
Should Black size 8 be weighted more heavily as the most realistic liquid comp because its ask is much lower than the rest of Black?
Do Supreme Dr. Martens historical comps justify holding one-year values near the high-$300s and low-$400s, or should the model fade harder after week one?
Should small sizes 6 and large size 13 be downgraded more aggressively when there is no active bid?

Black.basis: safest wearable color and strongest long-term liquidity prior; live StockX ask stack is high but spreads are very wide.

Snakeskin.basis: bolder release color with texture-driven demand; modeled near Black for most sizes, with size 13 treated as high-upside but thin due the widest spread.

6.basis: shoe-size prior plus live StockX earnMore. No completed-sale size ratios collected; wide spreads limit confidence.

horizonLogic.oneWeek: Day-1 values fade sharply for no-bid and very-wide-spread variants as launch scarcity cools and sellers undercut; Black 8 fades least because the day-1 anchor is already lower and more plausible.
horizonLogic.oneMonth: One-month values fade again as Week 10 attention moves on and buyers compare against older Supreme Dr. Martens comps rather than launch-day asks.
horizonLogic.oneYear: One-year values recover only for wearable Black core sizes and standout Snakeskin sizes, but remain below the most aggressive day-1 earnMore anchors unless the variant has a strong collectibility case.

liveMarketInterpretation.interpretation: Live StockX market data is uniformly bullish on asks/earnMore, but bid support is weak. Treat this as strong upside with thin liquidity rather than confirmed cleared resale performance.

confidenceAdjustment.basis: Live size-level data covers both colors and all modeled sizes, but completed sales are absent and most bid/ask spreads exceed $150.

colorLevelSummaries.reasoning: Black is the safe wearable pair and should have the broadest long-term audience, but the live StockX board is ask-led. Sizes 9 and 10 are the cleanest Black targets because they combine core sizing with strong earnMore values; size 8 is less explosive but more believable because its ask stack is much lower. Sizes 6 and 13 are maybe, not cop, because neither had an active bid at capture.

Snakeskin is the standout visual colorway and likely gets the strongest attention from buyers who want the collaboration to look different from inline Dr. Martens. Sizes 9 and 10 are the best balance of wearable sizing and upside. Size 13 has the highest earnMore value, but the $349 spread means it is high-upside only. Size 6 is downgraded to maybe because there was no active bid at capture.

overall.baseRecommendation: Selective cop for high-upside buyers; avoid treating ask-led prices as guaranteed exits.
keyRisks: Product-specific StockX public pages showed no visible completed-sale history at capture.
Most modeled variants have bid/ask spreads above $150, so day-1 values are heavily ask-led.
Black 6, Black 13, and Snakeskin 6 had no active highest bid at capture and are downgraded to maybe despite high calculated profit.
Snakeskin 13 has the highest projected profit but also the widest spread, making it a high-upside collector target rather than a liquid lock.
No eBay completed-sale data was collected for this pass.
Older Supreme Dr. Martens comps support upside but are low-volume, stale, or ask-side only depending on the comp.
Drop-week demand may be split with Ghostface items, limiting buyer depth for a $198 shoe after shipping and fees.
Supreme official page copy was captured for Snakeskin; Black relies on secondary product/source confirmation plus StockX.
